Well, welcome everyone. It's great to see you all. We're excited. We're all excited to be here. I wanted to start by saying a little bit about what the week will be about. It's going to be a lot of working together, a lot of exercises, a lot of writing, because that's really the best way to get better at writing is just to write. So we're here to help you with that. I know a lot of you aren't writers, and maybe a lot of you don't even ever want to be writers, or even maybe even ever do writing, but you still have to look at writing and say whether it's good, and you have to help the writers that you work with to improve what they've done, and to make it better, to make it be more effective. And if you can do it yourself, if you've had experience putting words on the page, that makes it you in a much better position to supervise the writers that you have to work with. So I wanted to say a couple of things. One is, writing to a large extent is not so much about technique. It's not about, okay, these rules, these are five things, these are two things to do, always do this, never do this. It's really about how you think. It's really about connecting to that part of you that already knows how to talk to people, that already knows how to persuade people, that already knows how to sell someone on something, that already knows how to tell them what a good product you have. So really what we're going to try to do here is not so much to fill your head with a lot of information about these are the things about writing, but to get you more connected with that part of you that if you sat down with a friend of yours and said, here's why my newsletter is so good, here's why you should subscribe to my newsletter, those same things that you would say to that friend would be what you would write that would make a really good letter to write. So I don't want to disappoint you if I don't give you a lot of rules and a lot of numbered things, do this, don't do this, because it's really about, again, connecting to that part of you that already knows how to write. But I think sometimes when we get in front of a piece of paper or we get in front of a keyboard to write, we become like a different person. We think, oh, it's got to sound like this, it's got to sound like everything else out there. But it doesn't, it's got to come from your heart and go out to their heart and connect with them. I wanted to start with structure, with outline, with maybe some formats that we can use to either structure the whole ad with, structure the whole promotion with, or just use to generate bits of copy within the ad. So these are some formulas and the basic one, has anyone heard of EIDA? Maybe it's, maybe it's, yeah, maybe it's different in German. But in English, it's A-I-D. And it's really very simple. It's basically, the first thing you want to do is to get, right, get attention. You know, you've got to, and I think this is where, the first place where a lot of advertising falls down, is you never get the reader's attention, right? You never drag the reader onto the page, it just starts out, it just starts out writing, you know, hello, my name is, you know, David Deutsch, I'm here to tell you about this thing, it's very interesting, but it's not like, hey, here's a way to make a million dollars, here's, you know, something to, to grab you and bring you in there. So the second one, after you've gotten their attention, you have to get their interest, R-E-S-T, which just means that you have to basically keep their attention. And I think the mistake a lot of ads make is they, they are written as if you're going to have the reader's attention all the way through. And the reader, it's almost like you have to look at the reader as always being ready to leave, right? Always being ready to go check their messages on their cell phone, check their email to go do something else. They don't want to be reading this thing that we've written for them. They want an excuse. And so, unless you're always saying, hey, this is interesting, more interesting stuff is coming up, here's a really interesting fact, here's some more interesting fact, here's something that really can make you a lot of money or save your health or whatever we're going to do for you, here's more for you, here's more for you. Unless you're constantly doing that, the reader just leaves. And of course, if the reader leaves, you don't sell them anything. A-I-A, so the next A, after you've gotten their interest, told them about what you've got, you know, is to... I think it's first to D and then the action. I think you're right. Desire. Great. So, you know, as I was funny, as I was doing it, I was going, wait a minute, what am I going to do when I get to here? This is, I'm in trouble. Right. So you've got their interest, right? They're interested in what you've got. But you've got to make it more than just them being interested in what you've got. You've got to make them that they want it, that it's the most important thing that they could have in their lives right now. That having what you've got will make their life better. It will enable them to do things that they want to do, whether that's to go on the vacation because they've made more money investing, whether it's to go out dancing because now their arthritis doesn't bother them anymore because the alternative health has cured them. It's whether it's to do their job as a secretary better or as a purchasing agent better. Whatever it is, you want to make that connection and show them how to desire what you have in order to fulfill everything else they want for their lives. And then finally, action. To take action. You want to convince them that now is the time to send you the request, the money, whatever it is that gets you or that gets them going with you. I think you don't really get them to send money, right? Does everyone have a send no money? You build them later. It's all soft offers, right? Okay. So it's very simple, but a lot of times we forget when we're writing something, we just kind of write and we don't really realize, hey, if you need to incorporate all these things. Another one is SSS. So star. And this is used a lot for infomercials where you'll have someone who's a spokesperson, a star, a billing maze or something like that. But even for what you do, for any product, the editor can be the star. Sometimes you can have a celebrity. Sometimes you can have the person. You can become the star in a way. If you become like a character, if you become interesting as the publisher writing to these people, you know, kind of like in America, Bill Bonner has become kind of a star. You know, he writes to people and they want to know about him and his chateau in France and his unique way of looking at the world. So you've got a star, a person, more personality driven. And then that star has a story. This is what I did. This is what my background is. This is what I can do for you. And then of course, that leads to the solution that is being offered. This enables you to make a lot of money. This enables you to improve your health, be a better purchasing manager or whatever it is. So again, this can be for all of the copy kind of like a way of organizing all of your copy, or it can just be for maybe there's one section of your copy that's got a little bit of a star, little some story and he's got a solution for you. And then the last one I wanted to go over with you is problem, right? You've got a problem. You don't make enough money. You're you have pain. You have pain from not being as good of a secretary as you might like or your bosses is giving you a hard time. Whatever it is, there's a problem of some sort. And what you want to do with that problem is not just to present the problem. But before you present the solution, you want to kind of press the problem a little bit. You want to make sure that you don't want to leave out that you want to help them understand that this is a more painful problem than maybe they thought, or you want to help them remember how painful it is, right? How painful it is that they're losing money in the stock market. Remember last year when it was so bad and you lost all that money, how bad you felt? You know, remember your arthritis pain, how painful it is that you can't do the things that you love to do. You really have to kind of remind them because when they start to read your letter, they're not really thinking about that necessarily. They're just kind of going through their day. So in order to get them to buy your product, to order your product, if they're not thinking about their pain and how bad it is, they're not going to have the desire to do that. So it's our job as copywriters to remind them that that this is important, that their pain is important, that their pain exists, and then that there's a solution for it. And the solution really has two parts a lot of times. Sometimes you have to be careful that the solution isn't, my product, look, my product is a solution. But sometimes the solution, there's a, so the product, so maybe the solution isn't your product first. The solution is to be better informed about the stock market. The solution is maybe homeopathy. The solution is alternative medicine, not to be, not to use the medicine from the drug companies that makes you sicker and has all these side effects. That's really the solution. And then the product is really what helps you get that solution more easily. Instead of you having to go out and research information about the stock market, then we do it for you. In fact, we have four people that you get that information from. Instead of you having to go find out, oh, how do I use alternative medicine? Medicine is this herb better than this herb. Where do I find good herbs? Where do I find what's the best thing for me? Oh, we have a newsletter for you that helps you to do that. So it's really important because when you bring in your product too soon, people kind of get resistant. They go, oh, I don't want another newsletter. Oh, this is about a newsletter. I'm not interested. But if you keep telling them things about a solution that don't include your product, the resistance doesn't come up. Does that make sense? I think a lot of times, and I know in in the financial world, especially, you won't know what the solution is for, you won't know what the product is until pretty far down in the letter. It talks about other, it talks about the solution more up front. It's very what we call indirect. And then the last one, which is something I learned from Joe Schrieffer over at Agora that he likes to use, is to build a promote, you can build a promotion or build large parts of a promotion simply by objections. Because after all, if a client, if a prospect has no objections to buying your product, they'll buy your product, right? If they don't object to that it's expensive or that it costs money, they don't object to that it's maybe hard to read. If they don't object to it's not for me or whatever, then they'll buy it. So to use that, just say, okay, first objection is that the product is expensive, it costs money. So you answer that objection. Objection number one, you answer blah, blah, blah, blah, blah, it doesn't really cost money, it makes you money. When you don't have arthritis, you'll be much more productive. It's worth any amount of money. Maybe the next objection is it's hard to use. I don't know how to make investments. I don't have a broker. I don't know what to do. I don't have a computer. So no, you answer that, that, that, that. So all this stuff then is your copy, right? Here's a little subhead, blah, blah, blah, it doesn't cost a lot. Subhead, so easy to use. And it's just a way of generating, you could generate a whole promotion just by thinking of different, you know, different objectives. I don't have time to do this and blah, blah, blah, blah, here's why it's easy, takes no time, 20 minutes a day is all you have to do. The first thing I wanted you to do for an exercise is to think about these different formats that we've gone over and think about how you can use them for your promotion that you want to work on. How can you make it, you know, the, being, answering objections. How can you make it being problem, more problem, pain, and then solution to that. How can you make it maybe a star in there or something like that? How can you do more, you know, AI, DA or AI, AD or whatever it was, you know, how can you be sure, did you really get their attention? Did you really have their interest? How can it be more attention getting, more interesting than what you've got if you came with something that you're working on? Or if you're starting from scratch, how can it be more attention getting? How can it be as interesting as possible?
